# Research cold-email drafts for Celina Lin

Review the employer posting and lab website before sending. Replace generic recipients with a specific PI or hiring manager when possible.

## lululemon — Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA

**Subject:** Interest in Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a50c6a45165966a1161bb6a

---

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. **Subject:** Interest in Research Assistant II/III - Immunobiology - Divanovic Lab — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Cincinnati Children's Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Assistant II/III - Immunobiology - Divanovic Lab opportunity with Cincinnati Children's at Cincinnati Children's.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## UL Solutions — Laboratory Technician - Fairfax, VA

**Subject:** Interest in Laboratory Technician - Fairfax, VA — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear UL Solutions Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Laboratory Technician - Fairfax, VA opportunity with UL Solutions at UL Solutions.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Golf, Tennis, and Sports Content Creators in Houston, TX / TeachMe.To — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear TeachMe.To Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Golf, Tennis, and Sports Content Creators in Houston, TX / TeachMe.To opportunity with TeachMe.To at TeachMe.To.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the HR Assistant opportunity with Saint Mary of Nazareth Hospital at Saint Mary of Nazareth Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the UTS - Temporary General Talent Pool at NC State and UNC Chapel Hill opportunity with North Carolina State University at North Carolina State University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## lululemon — Educator / 6th and Lamar

**Subject:** Interest in Educator / 6th and Lamar — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / 6th and Lamar opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Dual Language Immersion Teaching Assistant - PreK - 2026-2027 opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## SchoolSpring — 2026-2027 English Teacher @ Elsik HS

**Subject:** Interest in 2026-2027 English Teacher @ Elsik HS — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the 2026-2027 English Teacher @ Elsik HS opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear SGS Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Lab Analyst - multiple opportunities available! opportunity with SGS at SGS.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a454688c2d11a6a46668a1a

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-Time Administrative Assistant III (Training Division) opportunity with North Carolina Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services at North Carolina Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Florida Polytechnic University — Student Worker - Computer Engineering

**Subject:** Interest in Student Worker - Computer Engineering — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Florida Polytechnic University Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Student Worker - Computer Engineering opportunity with Florida Polytechnic University at Florida Polytechnic University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educational School-Based Content Specialist opportunity with United Activities Unlimited, Inc. at United Activities Unlimited, Inc..

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Henley Elementary School Paraprofessional- Title I — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Henley Elementary School Paraprofessional- Title I opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a4d7fedd27b2c4dda9b7e47

## SchoolSpring — Paraprofessional I (PT)- 268

**Subject:** Interest in Paraprofessional I (PT)- 268 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Paraprofessional I (PT)- 268 opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Pre-ETS Instructor - Hammond, IN — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Public Consulting Group Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Pre-ETS Instructor - Hammond, IN opportunity with Public Consulting Group at Public Consulting Group.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Montefiore Einstein Technology — Lab Assistant

**Subject:** Interest in Lab Assistant — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Montefiore Einstein Technology Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Lab Assistant opportunity with Montefiore Einstein Technology at Montefiore Einstein Technology.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Centralized Registration Technician Intern, Student, Community & Personnel Support - Short-term for 2026/2027 school year (June - September 2026) opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Program Coordinator opportunity with University of Maryland Center for Environmental Science at University of Maryland Center for Environmental Science.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

Dear Penn State University Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Women's Volleyball Analyst and Analytics Coordinator opportunity with Penn State University at Penn State University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am reaching out about the School Administrative Assistant, 4 hrs/day (Confluence High School) opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I am reaching out about the Research Technician (Institutional Research Analyst I), Institutional Research & Analytics-2 Positions opportunity with California State University-San Bernardino at California State University-San Bernardino.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Coordinator I/II/III Opportunities – Multiple Locations Nationwide opportunity with Headlands Research at Headlands Research.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## Soar Autism Center — Behavior Analyst in Training - Denver Metro Area

**Subject:** Interest in Behavior Analyst in Training - Denver Metro Area — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Soar Autism Center Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Behavior Analyst in Training - Denver Metro Area opportunity with Soar Autism Center at Soar Autism Center.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

**Subject:** Interest in Associate Training Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Werfen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Training Specialist opportunity with Werfen at Werfen.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## SchoolSpring — ECSE Paraprofessional -26 hrs/week- Klamath Early Childhood Development Center

**Subject:** Interest in ECSE Paraprofessional -26 hrs/week- Klamath Early Childhood Development Center — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the ECSE Paraprofessional -26 hrs/week- Klamath Early Childhood Development Center opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Summer Youth Development Professional — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Boys & Girls Clubs of Lower Bergen County Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Summer Youth Development Professional opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of Lower Bergen County at Boys & Girls Clubs of Lower Bergen County.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a35ddf729c90c607e4e3910

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Natera — EHS Administrator

**Subject:** Interest in EHS Administrator — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the EHS Administrator opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

More broadly, I have built reproducible genomics workflows and enjoy translating complex biological questions into clear, testable computational analyses.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Associate Clinical Oncology Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Clinical Oncology Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/6017918004

More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Field Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Field Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/6030732004

**Subject:** Interest in GTM Operations - Japan — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Cognition Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the GTM Operations - Japan opportunity with Cognition at Cognition.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/cognition/0116dd2e-5a6d-45c7-89e4-0ed5e467d8d6

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the IOP/PSC Team Lead, Socal | SW opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/6107805004

---

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Organ Health Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/5810414004

**Subject:** Interest in PSC Manager - Islip, NY — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the PSC Manager - Islip, NY opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. **Subject:** Interest in Study Delivery Associate — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Amgen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Study Delivery Associate opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. ## Amgen — Study Delivery Sr Associate

**Subject:** Interest in Study Delivery Sr Associate — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Amgen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Study Delivery Sr Associate opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://amgen.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/India---Hyderabad/XMLNAME--Study-Delivery-Sr-Associate--_R-238197

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin
